title: Query Understanding, Filters, Rewriting, Expansion, and Decomposition · Independent Program Trial
summary: An independent team receives a different corpus, query mix, relevance policy, language set, access model, latency budget, harm profile, and evidence duty and transfers query understanding, filters, rewriting, expansion, and decomposition into a new bounded query-plan compiler. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of bounded query-plan compiler with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Extract intent, entities, exact terms, time, language, tenant, source, and authorization filters through a validated query schema.
Preserve original query, rewrite purpose, version, generated alternatives, filters, and per-rewrite retrieval contribution.
Evaluate synonyms, acronyms, spelling, pseudo-relevance feedback, and domain vocabulary without drifting away from user intent.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""rag-retrieval-augmented-generation cumulative evidence-grounded retrieval portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python models and fixed fixtures only.
Native ML packages, files, models, vector stores, provider APIs, networks, credentials,
OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production effects require authorized transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RagEvidence:
    corpus_version: str
    document_id: str
    chunk_id: str
    query_id: str
    source_span: str


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RetrievalBoundary:
    tenant_id: str
    authorized: bool
    answerable: bool
    context_budget: int


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python RAG engineering portfolio ready"

steps.title: RAG and Python Commit to the mission model · Lab Rag Query Intent Entities Filters
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab RAG engineering team handles case 4922 during a multimodal maintenance-manual release. Build deterministic Python evidence to classify intent, validate filters, bound rewrites, preserve lineage, and ask when ambiguity remains; measure retrieval and generation separately before accepting an end-to-end score; then insert an untrusted instruction into a relevant document and prove it remains data. Browser code uses pure bounded models and fixed fixtures only. Native Python packages, embedding or generation models, vector databases, files, networks, credentials, provider APIs, OCR, load, faults, recovery systems, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

What must be established before implementation?

content.code:
# Evidence: rag-decomposition-clarification-budget-1xc6n3
# Competency: rag-decomposition-clarification-budget.
# Case 8042: a multimodal maintenance-manual release.
# Operating constraint: preserve source provenance, tenant authorization, deletion, freshness, citation offsets, and untrusted-data separation.
# Changed case: change tenant, language, date, and document version and reject stale or unauthorized evidence.
def evidence_rag_decomposition_clarification_budget_1xc6n3(query: str = "reset MFA for contractor", intent: str = "procedure", filters_valid: bool = True, ambiguity: int = 1, rewrite_count: int = 2, original_preserved: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1xc6n3 = "1xc6n3-3n6cx1-8042"
    assert evidence_variant_1xc6n3
    evidence_axes_1xc6n3 = (
        "corpus1xc6n3",
        "query3n6cx1",
        "tenant8042",
        "index1xc6n38042",
        "prompt3n6cx18042",
        "evaluation80421xc6n3",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1xc6n3)) == 6
    if not query.strip() or not intent:
        return False, 0, "query-intent"
    if not filters_valid:
        return False, rewrite_count, "filter-schema"
    if ambiguity < 0 or rewrite_count < 0 or rewrite_count > 5:
        return False, rewrite_count, "rewrite-budget"
    if not original_preserved:
        return False, rewrite_count, "query-lineage"
    if ambiguity > 2:
        return False, rewrite_count, "clarification-required"
    assert rewrite_count <= 5
    return True, rewrite_count, "bounded-query-plan"
